title: Recover M6 product gate entry prompt and reviewed M6 task DAG
summary: Recover the missing M6 entry prompt and reviewed M6 product-gate task DAG from the current roadmap, M5 closeout, Web release gate, platform matrix and engineering rules without starting M6 implementation.
acceptance: Current roadmap is recorded as authoritative and old M6/M7/M8/M9 inferences are explicitly deprecated
M6 is defined as Web / Windows Product Gate, M7 as Early Access / public playtest preparation and M8 as 1.0
coordination/reports/WM-0083-future-m6-entry-prompt.md exists and can be used as the future authoritative M6 entry prompt

Reviewed M6 product-gate DAG is drafted with task-sized work, dependencies, owners, reviewers, allowed paths, forbidden paths, validation commands, risks, owner gates, M0-M5 regression coverage and file ownership

M6 prompt and DAG cover Web gate, Windows Electron gate, performance, memory, loading, save/import/export, input, accessibility, packaging security, diagnostics/crash path, Web tier verdict and M7 readiness
No M6 implementation task is claimed, promoted, implemented or dispatched; no product runtime, simulation, content data or test behavior is changed

history.details: Created from owner authorization to recover the missing M6 product-gate entry prompt and reviewed DAG.
WM-0083 is done and the owner authorized the M6 entry recovery task; M6 implementation remains unstarted.

Recovered the missing M6 Web / Windows Product Gate entry prompt and reviewed DAG artifact from current roadmap, M5 closeout, Web release gate and platform matrix; no WM-0085+ task JSON or M6 implementation started; required gates pass.

verified: No blocking findings. Verified roadmap authority and deprecated old M6/M7/M8/M9 inference; M6 prompt and DAG define Web/Windows Product Gate, M7 Early Access/public playtest prep, M8 1.0; DAG covers Web, Windows Electron, performance, memory, loading, save/import/export, SAB fallback, input/accessibility, security, diagnostics, product verdict, closeout and M7 readiness; no WM-0085+ task JSON or implementation-path changes found; review gates passed.

Reviewer verified WM-0084; integration gates passed: validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check and pnpm quality with pnpm 11.8.0. M6 entry prompt and DAG artifact recovered; no WM-0085+ task JSON or implementation started.

objective: Create a reviewed M6 product-gate entry prompt and M6 task DAG artifact from current repository facts without starting M6 implementation.
forbiddenPaths: M6 implementation task claim promotion dispatch or execution
risksAndFileOwnership: Risk: old milestone assumptions leak into M6; ownership is limited to current roadmap and current closeout evidence.
Risk: creating downstream task JSON causes taskctl auto-promotion after WM-0084 done; WM-0084 drafts the M6 DAG as a reviewed artifact instead of instantiating WM-0085+ JSON.
Risk: M6 planning becomes implementation; forbidden paths block product, simulation, content and test behavior changes.
benchmarkImpact: No benchmark baseline or artifact changes. WM-0084 only defines future product-gate benchmark requirements and preserves existing 10 percent warning / 20 percent blocking thresholds.
reviewRouting: Project-director completes to reviewer. Reviewer must verify that M6 is Web / Windows Product Gate, the current roadmap is authoritative, no old M6/M7/M8/M9 inference was used, and no M6 implementation was started.
rollbackModel: Revert WM-0084 task/report artifacts, the recovered future M6 entry prompt and narrow project-state/roadmap markers if any; leave M5 closeout and M6 blocked.
ownerGate: Block if current roadmap and Web release gate are insufficient to define M6 without guessing
Block if product implementation, runtime code, simulation code, content data, tests, benchmark artifacts or package manifests need edits
Block if creating M6 task JSON would make taskctl promote M6 implementation tasks to ready before owner starts the M6 goal
Block if the prompt cannot preserve M0-M5 regression gates, M5 final hashes and existing benchmark thresholds
Do not start M7 or M8

sparkEligibility.reason: Milestone entry recovery, roadmap authority and product-gate DAG design require project-director ownership and independent reviewer verification.

review.summary: No blocking findings. Verified roadmap authority and deprecated old M6/M7/M8/M9 inference; M6 prompt and DAG define Web/Windows Product Gate, M7 Early Access/public playtest prep, M8 1.0; DAG covers Web, Windows Electron, performance, memory, loading, save/import/export, SAB fallback, input/accessibility, security, diagnostics, product verdict, closeout and M7 readiness; no WM-0085+ task JSON or implementation-path changes found; review gates passed.

integration.summary: Reviewer verified WM-0084; integration gates passed: validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check and pnpm quality with pnpm 11.8.0. M6 entry prompt and DAG artifact recovered; no WM-0085+ task JSON or implementation started.
